items.title: Salmon industry launches campaign to get 'truth of our industry out there'

children.content: A new advertising campaign featuring the stories of workers adjacent to Tasmania's fish farming industry is launched amid calls for the sector to be shut down.

gallery.altText: Two women stand side by side.
A drone shot of salmon pens in the water.
A couple pictured on a beach.

thumbnail.altText: Two women stand side by side.
A drone shot of salmon pens in the water.
A couple pictured on a beach.

items.title: Hobart to ditch hire e-scooters and bring in 'safer' e-bikes instead

children.content: The City of Hobart said e-bikes were a safer alternative to the e-scooters, which have continued to draw safety concerns from residents and visitors.

image.altText: Orange and purple e-scooters blocking a footpath

items.title: Greyhound racing ban kept alive as independent indicates support

children.content: Upper house independent Dean Harriss, who is up for re-election next month, says he will support the Tasmanian government's ban on greyhound racing, but only if the industry compensation package is increased.

image.altText: Serious Dean in grey suit, light tie sits in chambers, wooden paneling, benches, red upholstry.

items.title: Three weeks' pay guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers

children.content: A short-term rescue package announced by the Tasmanian and federal governments will guarantee the wages of workers at the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter.

items.title: 'Somewhere safe to go': Women's shelter to almost double capacity

children.content: A women's shelter struggling to meet enormous demand says it will soon be able to almost double its capacity.

image.altText: TBA

items.title: Charges over environmental impacts from cement ship crash with tugboats

children.content: The owner of a bulk cement carrier has been charged with environmental offences following a 2022 crash that sank two tugboats in north-west Tasmania.

image.altText: A tugboat being lifted by a crane.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

gallery.altText: A mix of cars and trucks fill four lanes of peak hour traffic on freeway.

thumbnail.altText: A mix of cars and trucks fill four lanes of peak hour traffic on freeway.

items.title: Iran's meme factory is using 'the weapons of their enemy' to embarrass Trump

children.content: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

image.altText: AI-generated image of Donald Trump animated in a lego-style with a terrified expression.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of a brunette woman wearing glasses.

items.title: Travel giant's $240m overcharging scandal balloons to claims of fake agreements

children.content: Brisbane-based Corporate Travel Management has detailed a laundry list of concerns about a ballooning scandal, including reliance on potentially fake documents.

image.altText: Corporate Travel Management website.

items.title: 'Lost everything': New Zealand's capital cleans up as rain pushes north

children.content: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

image.altText: People kneel into gutters to collect rocks and soil dislodged in heavy rain

items.title: How young boys helped Australians wage guerilla war against Japan in Timor

children.content: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

gallery.altText: A graphic shows a contemporary colour of a landscape in Timor with a black and white photo of a boy and soldier in the middle.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic shows a contemporary colour of a landscape in Timor with a black and white photo of a boy and soldier in the middle.

items.title: Case dropped against actor accused of 'breaking the girl code'

children.content: The actor was accused of grabbing the breast of a staffer at Parliament House in Melbourne during a pro-Palestine demonstration two years ago.

image.altText: A woman with long blonde hair in a peach and green dress walks along a city street with a man in a suit behind her.

items.title: Job cuts spark calls for WA's struggling coal miners to merge

children.content: As the fallout continues from Premier Coal's decision to cut up to 100 jobs from its Collie workforce, calls grow for it to be merged with Griffin Coal.

image.altText: Pit workings at Premier Coal's mine near Collie.

items.title: The growing problem stopping Melbourne residents using their microwaves

children.content: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

image.altText: Powerlines about a suburban street

items.title: Ampol has received $25m to produce renewable diesel, but will it boost supply?

children.content: The Queensland government has given $25 million to Ampol so it can produce renewable diesel in a bid to boost domestic fuel supplies.

image.altText: An industrial plant

items.title: 'I'm the worst cook in the house': How households make mealtimes easier

children.content: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

gallery.altText: Gemma Plunkett preparing a meal in her light-filled apartment kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: Gemma Plunkett preparing a meal in her light-filled apartment kitchen.

items.title: The Michael Jackson biopic is as generic as its subject was singular

children.content: Spanning from the Jackson 5's 1960s discovery through to the height of his fame in the late 80s, Michael skates over the more challenging aspects of the King of Pop.

image.altText: A man in a red jacket smiles at the camera while four other men stand behind him.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

image.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

items.title: NRL hit man who suffered 50 concussions says impact of new rules unclear

children.content: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads guilty over separate assault

children.content: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

image.altText: Brodie Dewar leaves court surrounded by reporters holding microphones.

items.title: $50/hour charge for older Australians to shower reversed by government after six months

children.content: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

image.altText: A close shot of a husband and wife holding each others hands.

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

articles.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A wet, damaged road has chunks of bitumen missing.

gallery.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

thumbnail.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: Staff face misconduct proceedings after foster kids allowed to live with killer

children.content: Some Department of Communities and Justice staff members will face misconduct proceedings after a bungle that saw two foster children living with triple killer Regina Arthurell earlier this year.

image.altText: A selfie picture of a person in blonde hard and big dark sunglasses

items.title: South Sydney might never get a better chance to end the longest NRL hoodoo

children.content: The Rabbitohs have never won a game in Melbourne in almost three decades of trying but they might never get a better chance than this weekend.

image.altText: A mascot in a superhero-type padded costume holds up a toy rabbit before a rugby league match.

items.title: NSW welcomes back superstar, hands Origin debut to Dragons gun

children.content: The Sky Blues welcome back Millie Elliott for the 2025 Women's State of Origin series opener, while Emma Verran's pregnancy opens up a spot for Teagan Berry to make her debut.

image.altText: NSW Sky Blues prop Millie Elliott runs past pyrotechnics and onto the field for a State of Origin game in the rain.

items.title: Aussie junior 'obsessed' with golf set to test his skills overseas

children.content: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

gallery.altText: A young boy sitting next to his golf clubs while on the course

thumbnail.altText: A young boy sitting next to his golf clubs while on the course

items.title: Vandalised war memorial soldier repaired in time for Anzac Day

children.content: A Laurieton stonemason rushed to the task of repairing a vandalised marble rifle on a NSW Mid North Coast war memorial.

image.altText: White marble statue of Australian soldier, resting rifle atop granite plinth in mid road intersection with mostly blue sky behin

items.title: Meet the eco-venture turning an invasive pest into dog treats

children.content: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

image.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

items.title: Recordings point to second Queensland population of rare night parrot

children.content: Ecologists say thousands of hours of audio recordings have detected night parrot calls in south-west Queensland.

gallery.altText: small bird on the ground beside bush.

thumbnail.altText: small bird on the ground beside bush.

items.title: 'I cannot afford to lose': Moloney's slow path back to boxing summit

children.content: Jason Moloney knows the path back to the summit of world boxing is paved with pain, but he will take another step back in Brisbane on Thursday night.

image.altText: Jason Moloney stands on the ring corner

items.title: In a split second, an abuse survivor made the 'surreal' decision to talk

children.content: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

image.altText: a woman with clasped hands on her lap

items.title: Group says site of new greyhound track should be for housing

children.content: The council and a community group fought against it, but Orange is set to get a new greyhound racing track after the project was approved by the Western Regional Planning Panel.

image.altText: Eight greyhounds race down a sand track at Cannington.

items.title: Central Australian community sues government over climate-unsafe housing

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say scorching outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they're suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

items.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting

children.content: US authorities are investigating if there was any criminal behaviour involving interactions between Open AI's ChatGPT app and a man accused of killing two people and injuring six others in a shooting at Florida State University.

image.altText: James Uthmeier standing in front of a microphone wearing a suit.

items.title: 'Terrifying' experience for WA family as storm tears roof off their home

children.content: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

image.altText: Letterbox reading '10' in the foreground with a gutted home in the background.
